items.title: When Bitcoin prices turned against Michael Saylor, he quietly pivoted to risky financial gambit at Strategy
items.description: Saylor has been offseting the Bitcoin drag by reverting to different and dangerous scheme: Issuing tons of preferred stock.

items.title: As Gates’ Epstein connection unnerves staff, his foundation looks for a way forward
items.description: The billionaire’s abrupt withdrawal from India’s AI Summit follows renewed scrutiny over his relations with Jeffrey Epstein.

items.title: Cuba is quickly nearing a point of no return as the U.S. weaponizes its Venezuelan oil supplies
items.description: A U.S. resolution in Cuba may not require full regime change, as the Cuban leadership rapidly runs out of options.

items.title: Troubled Nvidia ally Supermicro pledged to hire a new CFO ‘immediately.’ That was 14 months ago.
items.description: “No one wants this job,” Cowen Partners search firm president Shawn Cole said.

items.title: Former Prince Andrew was arrested. Bill Gates backed away from a speech. For these power players, the Epstein walls are closing in
items.description: Many cite the need to avoid further “distraction” as they step down.
